title: OpenGL vs Vulkan vs WebGPU
description: OpenGL hides state and synchronization; Vulkan exposes explicit pipelines, memory, and barriers; WebGPU is a safer explicit middle ground for the web.
body: Use when choosing or porting between graphics APIs. OpenGL is a global state machine that manages synchronization and memory for you, which is simple but hides hazards and costs; Vulkan exposes pipeline objects, explicit memory allocation, and manual barriers/synchronization for control and lower overhead at the cost of verbosity; WebGPU offers explicit pipelines and bind groups with validation built in for the web. Do not carry OpenGL's implicit-sync assumptions into Vulkan, where missing barriers cause corruption. Actions: match the API's synchronization and resource model when porting. Validation signals include a clean validation-layer run after a port. Risk boundary: implicit-to-explicit ports drop synchronization that the driver used to add; verify every hazard is now covered by a barrier.
